verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted member of the CRISP family expressed in the epididymis. It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and binds to the sperm surface — specifically the postacrosomal/equatorial segment involved in sperm-egg fusion. This surface association is restricted to sperm cells and the male reproductive tract context. The protein reaches the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane, qualifying as surface-accessible, but only in this narrow germline/reproductive tissue context. This maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface.
